title: seq-validate expected-value spec
description: The optional YAML spec passed to `seq-validate --spec <spec.yaml>`: the expected imaging metrics a sequence is asserted against. This is the input contract (the report is the output contract; see report-v1.schema.json). Validate a spec by converting the YAML document to JSON first — YAML maps cleanly (mappings to objects, sequences to arrays, null to null). The assertion policy is lenient: only the fields present are asserted, and every numeric field also accepts an explicit opt-out (the literal `none`, any case, or null). The tool itself tolerates unknown top-level keys — it warns rather than rejects — but this schema enumerates the recognized contract, so a key outside it is flagged here as it would be warned there. Put free-form authoring notes under `name`, `acquisition`, or `notes`.

te_ms.description: Effective (k-centre) echo time, in milliseconds. Default tolerance: abs 0.1 ms.

tr_ms.description: Repetition time, in milliseconds. Default tolerance: abs 0.1 ms.

flip_angle_deg.description: Excitation flip angle, in degrees. Default tolerance: rel 5%.

n_slices.description: Slice/slab count (distinct excitation frequency offsets). Default tolerance: exact.

echo_spacing_ms.description: Echo spacing, in milliseconds (echo trains only). Default tolerance: abs 0.1 ms.

fov_mm.description: Field of view as `[x, y, z]`, in millimetres (nominal/logical size; declared `oversampling` is divided out of the measured value before comparison). Up to 3 axes; a per-axis `none`/null leaves that axis unasserted. Default tolerance: rel 3% in-plane (x, y), rel 10% through-plane (z).

matrix.description: Acquisition matrix as `[x, y, z]` (nominal/logical size; declared `oversampling` is divided out of the measured value before comparison). Up to 3 axes; a per-axis `none`/null leaves that axis unasserted. Default tolerance: exact.

oversampling.description: Per-axis `[x, y, z]` oversampling factor, divided out of the measured geometry before comparison. Each provided axis must be a positive number. Up to 3 axes; absent / `none` / null means [1, 1, 1].

scanner.description: Scanner profile stem selecting the hardware/safety limits (e.g. `ge-premier`, `generic-3t`). An input, not an asserted field — it produces no `spec.*` result. `none`/null opts out.

tolerances.description: Per-field tolerance overrides, keyed by the asserted `spec.*` field name (note the per-axis suffixes: `fov_mm_x`, `matrix_y`, ...). A field absent here uses its built-in default (see each field's description).

name.description: Free-form sequence name. Ignored by the validator (never asserted).

acquisition.description: Free-form acquisition-description block. Ignored by the validator (never asserted).

notes.description: Free-form notes. Ignored by the validator (never asserted).

optOut.description: Explicit opt-out: YAML null or the literal `none` (any case). The field parses but is not asserted.

tolerance.description: A per-field tolerance band: `exact` (strict equality), `{abs: <n>}` (absolute band in the field's own unit), or `{rel: <n>}` (fraction of |expected|, e.g. 0.05 = 5%).

abs.description: Absolute band in the field's own unit.
